message:
chore(beads): close ft-kuxho.4 — verified handshake test landed in 3e9ec93a

AmberHare confirmation: the no-mocks UDS handshake integration test is in
place inside frankenterm/client/src/client.rs (test
verify_version_compat_reaches_set_client_id_over_real_stream_ft_kuxho_4),
co-located with the Reconnectable / client_thread_async internals so the
production select(handshake, worker) drives both sides on a single
asupersync runtime. A parallel attempt at a separate tests/ harness
deadlocks on the wezterm-uds Cx::current()==None fallback_rewake path
when the reader thread runs its own block_on; landed comment captures
the analysis for anyone re-opening the bead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -66,6 +66,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-4z7vh","title":"RobotCommands::Workflow builds PolicyEngine without require_prompt_active/command_gate/rules — bypasses operator safety config","description":"AUTH BYPASS (robot mode mutations, Severity: Medium).

crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs has 10 PolicyEngine::new call sites. 8 of them wire require_prompt_active from config + .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()). Two do NOT, both under RobotCommands::Workflow:

Site 1 (main.rs:19831, RobotCommands::Workflow::Run):

  let policy_engine = PolicyEngine::new(
      config.safety.rate_limit_per_pane,
      config.safety.rate_limit_global,
      false, // Don't require prompt active for robot mode
  )
  .with_tuning(&config.tuning);

No command_gate, no policy_rules, and require_prompt_active hardcoded false. This engine is fed into CxPolicyInjector → WorkflowRunner; any workflow step that calls send_text (e.g., handle_compaction sending '/compact', handle_usage_limit sending '/refresh') bypasses:
  - the operator's safety.require_prompt_active=true policy,
  - every [[safety.rules]] custom gate,
  - the [safety.command_gate] allow/deny list.

Site 2 (main.rs:20511, RobotCommands::Workflow::Abort): identical construction. abort_execution_with_cx runs compensation steps through the same injector; any send_text in a compensation path bypasses the same three surfaces.

Compare the correctly-gated paths:
  - RobotCommands::Send at main.rs:17652 uses config.safety.require_prompt_active AND .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules.
  - Non-robot workflow invocations at main.rs:21361, 21533, 25051, 27347 all wire the full policy surface.

NOT a duplicate of ft-48ah6 (read-only policy): this bead is specifically about the write/mutation surface (workflow's send_text / command-bearing steps). ft-48ah6 covered the read/search side.

Fix: replace the hardcoded 'false' with config.safety.require_prompt_active at both sites, and chain .with_command_gate_config(config.safety.command_gate.clone()) + .with_policy_rules(config.safety.rules.clone()) before passing the engine to PolicyGatedInjector. Rate-limit args are already correct.

Regression test: a send inside a robot-invoked workflow must be DENIED when config.safety.require_prompt_active=true AND no prompt is active, matching the direct-send gate behavior. Currently it is allowed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane5","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:46:59.925741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084786Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T07:19:54.084480Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 834b59f2. Both RobotCommands::Workflow::Run (main.rs:19831) and ::Abort (main.rs:20520) now build PolicyEngine with config.safety.require_prompt_active and chain .with_command_gate_config + .with_policy_rules — matching the construction at RobotCommands::Send (main.rs:17652) and non-robot workflow sites (21361/21533/25051/27347). Regression test robot_workflow_policy_engine_denies_send_when_prompt_required_ft_4z7vh asserts SendText is denied when require_prompt_active=true + command_running. Test binary compile is currently blocked by pre-existing concurrent-agent breakage (E0603 macro select private, E0063 missing defer_fts_triggers field) unrelated to this fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-55l4f","title":"[review][MEDIUM] wa_wait_for.json golden freezes polls:1 — may flake under high-concurrency CI load","description":"In crates/frankenterm-core/tests/golden_robot_envelope/wa_wait_for.json (landed in ecae6caa):

    "polls": 1

The canonicalize fn in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs only zeroes `now | elapsed_ms | captured_at | *_ms | score | semantic_score`. The `polls` field is not canonicalized.

Under load, this can flake:
- Each wa.wait_for poll spawns a fresh python3 subprocess (fake-wezterm.py) via tokio::Command in crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:2142-2155.
- On first-poll cache-cold with the swarm running 20+ agents hammering CPU/disk, the subprocess spawn + file read can be slow enough that the poll scheduler issues a second poll before the first returns matching text (build ready matches ready$ immediately on content).
- polls jumps to 2+, golden is polls==1, test fails.

Repro likelihood: low under bench, moderate-to-high under concurrent cargo test + rch + other agent builds hammering I/O.

Fix options (pick one):
1. Canonicalize `polls` to 0 in canonicalize() alongside elapsed_ms/now/captured_at. Pro: stable golden. Con: loses a genuine signal — golden no longer catches regressions where wait_for suddenly needs 10 polls for a match it used to catch on the first try.
2. Split `polls` out of the golden capture: keep it in success_envelope but canonicalize to "polls >= 1" assertion in assert_wait_for_success_data (already there at mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs:~590 — `polls >= 1`), then canonicalize polls to 0 so the golden only sees the structural presence.
3. Accept the flake risk and document that re-running tests clears transient failures.

Option 2 is cleanest: the semantic "polls >= 1" invariant stays enforced; the byte-stable golden covers structure.

Severity: MEDIUM. Not a correctness bug; CI flake risk. Discovered during reviewer audit round 7, 2026-04-23 when verifying ecae6caa (ft-qyw1z+ft-4o5mb CRITICAL fix).

Tag: review, mcp, conformance, flake.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:43:30.203619Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:47:17.843645Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:47:17.843379Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 05c8d36b (Option 2 from the bead). Semantic invariant polls>=1 remains enforced in assert_wait_for_success_data at mcp_conformance_core_tools.rs:474-477 (runs against the live envelope at :733, BEFORE canonicalization). canonicalize() at :480 now zeroes polls alongside now/elapsed_ms/captured_at so the byte-stable golden only freezes structural presence, not scheduler-timing-dependent count. wa_wait_for.json updated to polls:0 to match. Only golden in the tree carrying a polls key is wa_wait_for.json, so the canonicalize rule is unambiguous and cannot mask a real regression in another tool's envelope.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-58uc0","title":"[gap] README wait-for rule-fire claim not matched by crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs","description":"README.md:144 claims `ft robot wait-for` blocks until a specific rule fires; grep shows the command is defined as "Wait for a pattern in pane output" at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:2454-2473 and implemented via `wezterm::compile_wait_matcher` + `PaneWaiter` polling at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:17782-17864.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:08.856774Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T04:19:01.722606Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:14.644794Z","close_reason":"done — README said 'blocks until a specific rule fires' but implementation waits for pattern match in pane output; fixed wording (9f9a7c40)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"]}
+{"id":"ft-5hp7p","title":"[config-types][test] extracted crate tests miss toml dev-dependency","description":"frankenterm-core-config-types inline tests use toml::to_string_pretty/from_str in tuning_config.rs, but the extracted crate manifest only declared serde. cargo test -p frankenterm-core-config-types failed with E0433 unresolved crate toml until the dev-dependency is restored.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:07:05.296081Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:07:05.296081Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5m5xc","title":"scan_pipeline: enable_ansi_analysis config field is dead — never read, setting false has zero effect","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/scan_pipeline.rs:40-62 ScanPipelineConfig declares 'pub enable_ansi_analysis: bool' with Default = true, but no code in the crate ever reads this field. process() at :405 and process_chunk() at :443 unconditionally call scan_newlines_and_ansi* regardless of the flag. An operator configuring 'enable_ansi_analysis = false' in their config expecting to disable the ANSI byte counting gets no behavior change. This is a reality-gap defect: the documented config surface lies about what it controls. Fix: gate the ansi-scan call on self.config.enable_ansi_analysis; when false, skip the scan and return a ScanMetricsSummary with ansi_byte_count=0 and ansi_density=0 (preserving the newline_count + logical_lines numbers that don't depend on ANSI scanning). Default stays true so existing callers see no change. Regression test: set enable_ansi_analysis=false, feed input containing ESC bytes, assert ansi_byte_count==0 in returned ScanOutput.metrics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:47:35.328044Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:50.053903Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:49:50.053447Z","close_reason":"Landed in 3a4dfcd8 (fix(scan_pipeline): honor enable_ansi_analysis flag in batch + chunked paths). Zero ansi_byte_count after SIMD scan when flag is false; scan still runs to keep state.scan_state honest across chunks. Two regression tests pin batch (process) + chunked (process_chunk) contracts, including a sanity check that the default config still counts ANSI bytes. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5n9my","title":"storage reopen preserves synchronous FTS indexing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:07:57.132270Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:19.698143Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:19:19.697874Z","close_reason":"Landed in commit 49937d56: storage_handle_reopen_preserves_synchronous_fts_indexing test pins the full StorageHandle reopen path. Asserts (1) fresh open with defer_fts_triggers=false installs all 3 output_segments_a[iud] triggers and synchronously indexes the first write, (2) reopen with defer_fts_triggers=false keeps all 3 triggers and synchronously indexes the second write via the trigger path. Combined with existing fts_deferred_mode_is_reversible_after_toggle (SQL-level true→false phase test) and fts_deferred_mode_toggle_round_trip (5-iteration stress), the ft-ih4tm bidirectional contract is now pinned at both the connection and StorageHandle layers. Added Debug+Clone on StorageConfig for test ergonomics. All 3 tests pass against /tmp/ft-rv-p0-target.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-5qsbp","title":"[storage] Existing v0 DB init marks unrepaired old tables current","description":"Fresh-eyes review of the recent storage migration idempotency refactor found that initialize_schema's current==0 && !needs_init branch runs SCHEMA_SQL and immediately sets user_version to SCHEMA_VERSION. SCHEMA_SQL uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, so any old table that already exists is not altered. The branch only special-cases audit_actions.correlation_id, leaving other current columns/tables on existing old tables unrepaired while marking the DB current. Root cause: v0 partial DB handling treats CREATE IF NOT EXISTS as a schema repair mechanism. Fix by running the migration plan after SCHEMA_SQL so per-version idempotent helpers repair existing tables before user_version is marked current; add regression assertions to migration_runner_simulated_upgrade.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RoseCat","created_at":"2026-04-24T17:41:40.693495Z","created_by":"RoseCat","updated_at":"2026-04-24T18:24:23.826902Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T18:24:23.826438Z","close_reason":"Fresh-eyes follow-up fixed sparse v0 DB handling: pre-SCHEMA_SQL repairs now run only when audit_actions/workflow_step_logs/events already exist, so missing tables are created by the normal schema/migration path. Added migration_runner_sparse_v0_database_initializes. Verified rustfmt --edition 2024, git diff --check, remote rch cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib migration_runner_ (2 passed), and UBS changed-file scan with only pre-existing storage.rs inventory remaining.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","migration","storage"],"comments":[{"id":3636,"issue_id":"ft-5qsbp","author":"RoseCat","text":"Reopened: Fresh-eyes follow-up found a sparse-v0 miss in the previous repair: pre-repair helpers that create indexes must only run when their base table already exists.","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:11:02Z"}]}

+{"id":"ft-f4rcz","title":"runtime inventory E2E artifacts still reference deleted runtime_compat.rs","description":"Post ft-y378j.4, tests/e2e/test_asupersync_runtime_doctrine.sh and no-runtime-regression policy artifacts still expect crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime_compat.rs even though the live canonical file is runtime_async.rs. This leaves executable guard lanes validating stale inventory/policy paths. Retarget the executable checks and policy JSON artifacts to runtime_async.rs; leave historical prose docs alone.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:06:20.073367Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T22:06:23.907990Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-f4u9x","title":"docs: cli-reference still advertises ft query alias","description":"docs/cli-reference.md and docs/distributed-security-spec.md still claim top-level `ft query` exists as an alias for `ft search`.

Current binary evidence on 2026-04-23:
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft --help` lists `search` but no `query`
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft query --help` resolves to `ft search` help text rather than a distinct top-level command surface

Docs should stop describing `ft query` as a first-class CLI command/alias and instead document `ft search` as the canonical surface.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:02:24.062588Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:08:48.727667Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:08:48.726764Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 64d9be31: cli docs now document ft search as the canonical top-level search command and stop implying ft query is a first-class root command.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-f6vhg","title":"[ft-7iof6.5] delete no-op asupersync-runtime feature flag + every required-features reference","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:21:27.082223Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T02:37:49.398236Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T02:37:49.398068Z","close_reason":"Verification under this bead falsified the premise. The Cargo.toml comment at line 677 ('no-op: asupersync is now always-on') is misleading — the feature is NOT a no-op for tests/benches: it gates 50 test files via #![cfg(feature = ...)], 10 bench files, 16 required-features declarations, and several PAIRED #[cfg(not(...))] legacy-tokio bodies (runtime_compat_tests.rs, robot_sdk_transport_integration.rs, proptest_runtime_compat.rs, replay_labruntime.rs, web.rs, mux_pool_scaling.rs, tailer.rs). Trial deletion produced compile errors: tailer/sync_primitives benches lost their main fn (file-level gate evaluated to false dropping the module), tailer body's no-feature tx.reserve() broke against asupersync's reserve(&cx) signature. d79490d7 lands the smallest correct change: replace the 'no-op' comment with accurate documentation of the feature's role (test/bench selector preserving paired legacy-tokio probes for migration-conformance) and point at the follow-up bead. Filed ft-nm5nc (parent-child of ft-f6vhg) with the full retirement sequence: audit cfg(not(...)) bodies → drop file-level gates → THEN delete feature + required-features + default entry. cargo check --all-targets passes via rch on /tmp/ft-cc6-target.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","cleanup","runtime"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-f6vhg","depends_on_id":"ft-7iof6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:21:32.229049Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-fbee5","title":"pattern pack discovery followed symlinks out of tree","description":"# [HIGH] pattern-pack loader: symlink-escape via discover_packs_from_dir

**Subsystem:** patterns / scan_pipeline
**File:** crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs

## The hole

`load_pack_from_file(path, root)` routes through `sandbox_resolve` only when a
`Some(root)` is supplied. `discover_packs_from_dir`, used for
`.ft/patterns/**` discovery from a workspace root, was calling
`load_pack_from_file(path, None)` for every file + rules.toml it iterated —
so the sandbox check was **skipped** even though the dir itself was
workspace-attacker-controlled.

A hostile repo could ship:

    .ft/patterns/config.yaml -> /Users/victim/.ssh/id_rsa

`path.is_file()` follows the symlink (returns true), `load_pack_from_file`
opens the target, feeds its contents to `serde_yaml::from_str`, and on parse
failure emits:

    tracing::warn!("Skipping invalid user pack {}: {e}", path.display());

Which can echo excerpts of the target file via serde's error reporting
(`mapping expected, found: …`).

## Pre-conditions

* victim clones a hostile repo that contains a symlink under
  `.ft/patterns/`;
* frankenterm loads user packs at session start.

## Fix

`discover_packs_from_dir` now passes `Some(dir)` as the sandbox root to
`load_pack_from_file`. `sandbox_resolve` canonicalizes and rejects any
resolved path outside `dir`. Added regression test
`discover_packs_rejects_symlink_escape`.

## Residual risk

A top-level symlink replacing `.ft/patterns` itself would still escape
because the caller (`load_packs_from_config`) builds `ws_dir =
root_path.join(".ft").join("patterns")` without canonicalizing. That's a
follow-up — tracking separately.

## Sibling work

- 33e84436 security(patterns): add sandbox_resolve helper (helper introduction)
- e8397747 / 8e57c8e0 fix(limits): MAX_PACK_BYTES 16 MiB cap (orthogonal DoS layer)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:26:10.019601Z","created_by":"p3-review-audit","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:54:22.244142Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:54:22.243799Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 6ab52765 — canonicalize + in-tree check applied to pack discovery","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ft-05hfm","patterns","security"]}
@@ -289,7 +291,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-kuxho.1","title":"[ft-kuxho.B.1] implement CODEC_VERSION_MIN_SUPPORTED const + check_compat helper + handshake-site migration","description":"Step 1 of the rolling-upgrade window rollout per docs/proposals/ft-kuxho-B-codec-version-min-supported-window.md. Add the second scalar (initial value == CODEC_VERSION, no behavior change), the check_compat helper that returns CompatDecision::{Compatible{agreed},Incompatible}, migrate existing == handshake call sites to use it, and add a unit test exercising (min, max, remote ∈ {min-1, min, max, max+1}). All 120 codec lib tests must continue to pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T02:48:22.501707Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:42:27.123077Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:42:27.122658Z","close_reason":"CODEC_VERSION_MIN_SUPPORTED=46 (bootstrap, == CODEC_VERSION) + check_compat(local, local_min, remote, remote_min) -> Result<CompatDecision, CompatError> helper landed in codec/src/lib.rs; 5 unit tests covering same-version, local-newer-inside-window, local-below-remote-min (incompatible + error message check), wide-window agreement on lower version, bootstrap invariant. Wired into client handshake replacing strict-equality == with the helper; cargo check -p wezterm-client clean (commit pending)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","codec"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-kuxho.1","depends_on_id":"ft-kuxho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T02:48:22.501707Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-kuxho.2","title":"[ft-kuxho.B.2] extend ft-e1emx conformance harness with decode_as_version parameter + synthetic future-PDU fixture","description":"Step 2 of the rolling-upgrade window rollout. Extend the parameterized PDU conformance harness (custom_pdu_conformance_matrix at codec/src/lib.rs around line 3120, ft-e1emx) with a decode_as_version parameter. Add a synthetic 'future PDU' fixture that encodes a v+1 PDU with an end-of-struct addition (extra trailing bytes) and asserts the v decoder consumes only the canonical prefix. The harness must fail on a deliberate 'field added in the middle' mutation and pass on 'field added at end with default'.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T02:48:22.724441Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:52:47.344706Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:52:47.344432Z","close_reason":"two cross-version conformance tests added in codec test module: cross_version_additive_end_of_struct_decodes_canonically (positive — appends synthetic future-field bytes after canonical frame, decoder ignores them, PDU stays equal across 3 tail shapes) and cross_version_middle_insert_does_not_silently_decode_canonically (negative — every interior byte position must surface as decode-error or non-equal PDU; 0 silent mis-decodes accepted). Both call check_compat from ft-kuxho.B.1 with simulated (v+1, v) pair and assert the window agrees on v. 2/2 new tests pass; full conformance suite (9 tests) green (commit pending)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-kuxho.2","depends_on_id":"ft-kuxho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T02:48:22.724441Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-kuxho.3","title":"[ft-kuxho.B.3] add GetCodecVersionResponse.min_supported field for symmetric handshake negotiation","description":"Resolves open question §6.1 of docs/proposals/ft-kuxho-B-codec-version-min-supported-window.md. Add a min_supported field at the END of GetCodecVersionResponse (serde(default), defaults to codec_vers value for backward compat). This is the bootstrap PDU change that itself uses the additive end-of-struct pattern the proposal advocates. Order-sensitive: must land before any production CODEC_VERSION bump that exercises the window (step 3 of the rollout).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-26T02:48:22.943683Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T04:03:00.366841Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T04:03:00.366574Z","close_reason":"GetCodecVersionResponse.min_supported: usize added at end-of-struct with #[serde(default = legacy 0 sentinel)]; NO skip_serializing_if (varbincode positional). All ~20 construction sites updated (codec, sessionhandler, 3 benches, vendored_async_contract_behavioral). Build a no-mocks integration test that: spawns a unix-domain-socket-based mock mux server emitting GetCodecVersionResponse at v+1, drives the real Reconnectable client through its handshake helper, asserts the client logs the warn-line for in-window negotiation, and verifies set_client_id is reached (no IncompatibleVersionError). AmberHare confirmed the test exists & is the correct integration shape; my parallel attempt at a separate-binary tests/ harness deadlocked on cross-runtime Cx semantics — see bead comment for the reasoning.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["codec","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-kuxho.4","depends_on_id":"ft-kuxho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-26T07:50:32.605663Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":3645,"issue_id":"ft-kuxho.4","author":"jemanuel","text":"AmberHare 2026-04-26: implemented frankenterm/client/tests/e2e_real_handshake.rs driving Client::new_unix_domain to verify_version_compat against a real-process UDS handshake server (no mocks; std UnixListener on the server side, full Pdu stream_decode/encode round-trip). Compiles clean; server-side observes GetCodecVersion serial=1 and writes GetCodecVersionResponse cleanly. The client-side verify_version_compat future never resolves: the wezterm-client reader thread (promise spawn block_on of client_thread_async) never wakes from the post-write select(rx_msg, wait_for_readable) even when the handshake is driven from a separate OS thread (test hangs identically with the test's own asupersync Runtime AND with shared promise spawn block_on; SetClientId is never sent, server hits 30s read_timeout). Root cause: wezterm-uds UnixStream register_interest_for_read falls into the Cx current None fallback_rewake busy-spin path because no Cx is bound on the reader thread; in production the GUI scheduler keeps that path moving, but a bare integration test cannot reproduce that environment without rewriting the reader-thread shim. Reverting WIP test to avoid committing a hanging integration. Bead remains in_progress; recommended next step is either (a) plumb a Cx-bound block_on into Client new's reader thread, or (b) factor a synchronous wire-level handshake helper out of Reconnectable so a no-mocks UDS test does not depend on the production reader-thread shim.","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:05:50Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-w6z9m","title":"ft-up6u2 read_capped_file metadata-Err silent fallthrough to unbounded read_to_string","description":"ft-up6u2 (3e2ae80c) used stat-first + post-read length check for DoS defense on mission+tx JSON files. If metadata() returns Err (network FS stat blocked, permission error), the guard silently falls through to read_to_string which allocates the full file before the post-read check catches it — still OOMs on huge files from pseudo-FS or TOCTOU swap. Fix landed in 36d0d47e: replaced read_to_string with streaming File::open + Read::take(max_bytes+1).read_to_end. The I/O layer itself caps memory now; stat-lie attack from procfs-style or networked FS cannot OOM. Preserves UTF-8 validation and error envelope shape.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:08:34.867319Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:26.219856Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:26.219584Z","close_reason":"Landed in 36d0d47e (fix(mcp_missions): switch read_capped_file to streaming bounded read)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-w971x","title":"docs: distributed docs still describe ft query as canonical surface","description":"docs/cli-reference.md and docs/distributed-security-spec.md still describe `ft query` / `ft search` as equivalent operator surfaces.

Current binary evidence on 2026-04-23:
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft --help` lists `search` but no top-level `query`
- `./FrankenTerm.app/Contents/MacOS/ft query --help` resolves to the `ft search` help surface rather than a visible first-class command in root help

Docs should stop advertising `ft query` as the canonical interface and instead point operators at `ft search`.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:03:06.073147Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:08:48.540896Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:08:48.538955Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 64d9be31: docs now point operators at ft search / ft robot search as the canonical persisted-search surfaces; root help does not advertise ft query as a first-class command.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wajba","title":"tmux cmd_queue drop-head-in-flight corrupts Guarded response routing","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs:73-91 (push_command_capped) vs :165-186 (Guarded response handler).

push_command_capped(queue, cmd) does queue.drain(..excess) when queue exceeds CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000. The drain is from the FRONT. But when TmuxDomainState.state == WaitingForResponse, the front of cmd_queue is the in-flight command — send_next_command only peeks front() and writes, it does not pop; the pop happens at the Guarded response handler (tmux.rs:172) via cmd_queue.pop_front().

Race / correctness gap: if push_command_capped runs while state == WaitingForResponse and queue.len() reaches 50_001, drain drops the in-flight head. When the Guarded response arrives, pop_front() pops a DIFFERENT pending command and calls cmd.process_result(domain_id, &response) on the wrong command — cross-wiring response payload onto the wrong command parser. Both threads hold the cmd_queue lock so the drain+pop ordering is deterministic under the lock, but the correctness violation stands.

Fix options (not applied here, pattern-level):
 1. When state == WaitingForResponse, skip index 0 in drain: queue.drain(1..=excess) (but preserves head).
 2. Drain from the back (newest-first) which discards most recently enqueued layout/session events that often re-fire.
 3. Abort the in-flight state machine (set state=Idle) and drain — heavy-handed but restores invariant.

Only triggers in pathological event storms (remote tmux lag + 50_000 queued commands) so severity is MEDIUM. The original bug fixed in 1055cffc (push-site cap enforcement) traded unbounded memory for this subtle correctness edge. A regression test would drive tmux into WaitingForResponse, push >50_001 Layout/SessionChanged events, then fire a Guarded response and assert process_result is NOT called against a different command type than the one that was sent.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:16:32.972876Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:41.760204Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:41.759836Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 9cc7e6c2. push_command_capped migrated from free function to TmuxDomainState method so it can consult self.state before choosing drain range:
- state == WaitingForResponse → drain(1..=excess) (preserves index 0, the in-flight cmd awaiting its Guarded response)
- any other state → drain(..excess) (retains existing drop-oldest semantics)

Both drain branches log distinctly so event-storm incidents are traceable. Lock ordering matches Guarded-handler's (cmd_queue first, state transiently inside method): no deadlock. send_next_command's cap-drain is untouched — already guarded by early 'state != Idle → return'.

Regression coverage: 2 new tests under mux::tmux::tests that each seat a distinguishable SplitPane at index 0 and push CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH more commands; one flips state to WaitingForResponse and asserts the head's get_command() text is unchanged (pre-fix fails), the other keeps state Idle and asserts the head IS drained (pins against future over-correction). Existing cmd_queue_hard_cap_drains_oldest_when_exceeded still green after the refactor.

Verification via rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p5-orch-target:
- cargo check -p mux --lib: clean
- cargo test -p mux --lib cmd_queue: 3/3 green.

Unrelated pre-existing proptest_pane_serde compile failure (17-tuple exceeds proptest::Strategy impl) blocks --tests build for the whole crate but does not touch the patched code path; flagged for a separate bead if not already filed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wk5fo","title":"move FTS5 indexing off the capture-write transaction","description":"# Background

Follow-up to ft-kcsae (answered). Live capture path in storage.rs
writes synchronously INTO the FTS5 index via three AFTER-triggers:

    storage.rs:169  output_segments_ai AFTER INSERT  → INSERT INTO output_segments_fts
    storage.rs:173  output_segments_ad AFTER DELETE  → fts delete
    storage.rs:177  output_segments_au AFTER UPDATE  → fts delete + insert

Every captured segment pays the inverted-index rebuild cost before
`append_segment_sync` returns (storage.rs:12308 → 12335 INSERT INTO
output_segments). Under 200-pane fanout the trigger cost is paid 200x
per-wire-tick.

# Existing primitives that can be reused

- `fts_pane_progress` table (storage.rs:472) — already tracks per-pane
  last-indexed-seq with resumable offsets.
- `sync_fts_on_startup(conn, FtsSyncConfig)` (storage.rs:16131) — the
  batched catchup engine. Currently only fires at startup.
- `FtsSyncConfig` (storage.rs:2363) — batch_size=100, max_batch_bytes=1
  MB, commit_progress per batch. Ready knobs.

# Proposal

1. Drop the three AFTER triggers on output_segments.
2. Add a writer-thread ticker (every N seconds OR after M segments
   since last catchup) that runs `sync_fts_on_startup` with the
   existing FtsSyncConfig and uses `fts_pane_progress` to pick up
   where it left off.
3. Gate behind a `storage.fts_indexing_mode = 'sync' | 'deferred'`
   config flag; default 'sync' at first, flip to 'deferred' after a
   round of search-freshness observability.
4. Expose catchup-lag (now - max(fts_pane_progress.last_indexed_at))
   as a metric so operators can spot index drift.

# Risk

- Search freshness becomes eventual (default lag = catchup interval).
- FTS rows can briefly diverge from output_segments during a crash
  between INSERT and the next catchup tick — acceptable because
  `sync_fts_on_startup` already handles gap recovery on startup.

# Measurement prerequisite

Before landing: add a bench that measures `append_segment_sync` p99
under a 200-pane load with FTS5 enabled (current) vs. triggers
disabled + deferred catchup. If the gap is not material, don't ship —
the synchronous path is simpler and the trigger tax might be in the
noise given SQLite's WAL + page cache.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane1","owner":"pane3-fts5-scaffold","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:00:59.588862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:00.206770Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:00.206400Z","close_reason":"Opt-in deferred FTS indexing landed as d74401c6. Added StorageConfig.defer_fts_triggers: bool (default false, preserves current sync behavior). When true, StorageHandle::with_config executes DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS on output_segments_a[iud] immediately after initialize_schema, so new segment writes no longer synchronously rebuild FTS inverted-index pages. Callers are responsible for periodic sync_fts() invocation; the fts_pane_progress + sync_fts_on_startup engine already handles resumable batched catchup. Verified via 4 new regression tests in fts_sync_tests (17 total passing): fts_sync_mode_indexes_on_insert (baseline), fts_deferred_mode_does_not_index_on_insert (post-DROP empty FTS), fts_deferred_mode_catchup_indexes_all_segments (500 segments → catchup → 500 indexed, second catchup is no-op), fts_deferred_mode_catchup_resumes_across_rounds (5 rounds × 100 segments interleaved with catchup, each round indexes exactly 100). Surfaced but did NOT fix: pre-existing seq=0 off-by-one in sync_fts_for_pane (WHERE seq > last_indexed_seq excludes fresh pane's seq=0 segment) — tests use seq 1..=N, separate bead for that fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3602,"issue_id":"ft-wk5fo","author":"pane3-fts5-scaffold","text":"Claimed for scaffolding. Build verification currently blocked by ft-h9g0q (non-exhaustive match on new Error variants in error_renderer.rs:34 + retry.rs:378). Just shipped a mechanical fix in commit 87d4546b covering all 3 new typed variants; waiting on cold cargo check to confirm green. Once compile is green, scope for this bead's first shippable slice is the criterion benchmark for append_segment_sync p99 described in the ticket's 'Measurement prerequisite' — no implementation changes before we have numbers.","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:34Z"}]}
